item.title: SSH private key orphaned on disk when firewall creation fails during provisioning
item.intent: When provisioning fails at the firewall step, the SSH private key written to disk must be deleted — no private key file should survive a failed provision regardless of which step failed.
item.description: In src/cloud/provisioning/engine.ts, generateSshKeypair() is called at line 121 (before any API calls). If firewall creation fails at line 195-224, the engine destroys the VM and returns early at line 220-224. At this point, the instance has never been added to the registry (addInstance at line 229 has not run), so the keypair at deployDir/keys/<instanceId>.pem is orphaned — it can never be cleaned up by destroyInstance() because the registry has no record of it. Fix: on any early return after generateSshKeypair() succeeds (firewall fail, and the no-IP path at line 169-188 when IP is pending), delete the private key file with rmSync(keypair.privateKeyPath, { force: true }) before returning the error result. Alternatively, always add the instance to registry immediately after keypair generation, before the API calls.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/engine.ts lines 119-230 — confirm generateSshKeypair() is called before firewall, and that firewall failure returns without deleting the key
Identify all early-return paths after keypair generation: (a) firewall fail at ~line 220-224, (b) no-IP path at ~line 169-188 (key is registered there, skip cleanup)
In the firewall failure path (~line 207-224), add rmSync(keypair.privateKeyPath, { force: true }) before both return statements that exit after VM destroy
Run provisioning engine tests to confirm no regressions — check that the key file is absent after a simulated firewall failure
acceptanceCriteria: After a simulated firewall creation failure, the SSH private key file at deployDir/keys/<instanceId>.pem does not exist on disk
Successful provisioning still produces the key file and records sshKeyPath in the registry
The no-IP path (instance added to registry with sshKeyPath) is unchanged — key is not deleted in that path
